signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Geopolitical + Volatility
signals.text: USD/JPY trades at 163.35, holding above its SMA20/50/200 cluster following hawkish Fed rhetoric from Chair Warsh and rising Middle East tensions. A +275bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage remains the primary structural driver, though rising energy prices and Secretary Rubio’s comments on Iran introduce idiosyncratic pressure on JPY as an energy importer. The options market is aggressively pricing tail risk with a +3.5% Vol Risk Premium (IV 7.2% vs HV30 3.7%) and a moderate -1.45 25d RR put skew, signaling institutional demand for downside protection despite the spot uptrend. CFTC data shows a crowded JPY net short of -105,238, leaving the pair vulnerable to a disorderly squeeze if geopolitical risk forces a broader risk-off deleveraging. Carry dominates the immediate regime while VIX remains at 17.7 (normal volatility), but the 160.00-163.00 zone remains sensitive to BoJ verbal intervention. Risk: VIX breaks above 25, triggering a rapid unwinding of the yen-funded carry trade.

signals.title: GBP/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3368 is the locus of this week's dominant G10 trend, with USD outperforming GBP by a 0.892% spread across the G10 scorecard. While the nominal carry is neutral (Fed 3.75% vs BoE 3.75%), the USD real rate of +1.47% offers a significant 69bp premium over the GBP real rate of +0.78% due to lower US inflation expectations. Recent gilt yield surges have ramped up BoE hike bets, yet GBP remains the G10 laggard with a -0.396% average weekly return and a negative CESI score of -7. Price action is currently testing the SMA20/50 support cluster at 1.3362/1.3372; a sustained close below this level would confirm the bilateral weakness theme. The options market confirms this caution with a -0.66 25d RR put skew and an elevated +4.6% VRP. Risk: BoE surprises with hawkish guidance on July 30, forcing an unwind of the USD bilateral lead.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Policy + Positioning Divergence
signals.text: EUR/USD trades at 1.1413 ahead of today's ECB interest rate decision, positioned below its SMA20/50/200 stack. While the market bias leans toward a hold, hawkish expectations persist among analysts, creating a tactical divergence against the persistent data misses reflected in the EUR CESI of -18. Speculative positioning is notably stretched with COT LF net short at -74,637 (33.3% long), suggesting the market is already positioned for a dovish outcome or continued economic underperformance. The 135bp USD carry advantage (SOFR 3.61% vs €STR 2.18%) structurally limits upside, though an ATM IV of 7.1% vs HV30 of 4.7% indicates a 2.4% vol premium as traders hedge for an ECB surprise. A failure to deliver hawkish guidance today likely validates the SMA-confirmed downtrend toward 1.1350. ECB holds at 2.40%.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hawkish Bias + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD is holding at 0.7003, supported by a strong Australian labor market beat of 76,000 jobs and a subsequent hawkish shift in RBA expectations. The pair benefits from a -60bp carry advantage for AUD (Fed 3.75% vs RBA 4.35%) and a positive net speculative position of +21,946. Retail sentiment provides a contrarian tailwind, with 66% of retail traders currently short, diverging from the institutional long bias. However, this bullish macro setup is tempered by a negative CESI of -16, highlighting that the broader data trend remains weak despite the recent jobs surprise. Price remains pinned between the SMA20 (0.695) and SMA50 (0.7036), suggesting a consolidation phase until the RBA meeting on August 11. Risk: WTI Oil prices reverse lower, undermining the commodity-linked support for the AUD.
